title: Where Rain First Falls

features.name: dust-on-window
features.desc-on-touch: You're reading a memory from a train carriage: warm light descending, dust raised by wind and heat, the exact moment before rain. The archive keeps this as civic record.

features.name: first-gathering
features.desc-on-touch: Where rain learns it will gather. Vapor becomes drop becomes stream. The cistern below counts how many versions of wet it will hold.

features.name: meteorology-station
features.desc-on-touch: A collecting point for atmospheric measurements. The archive keeps every reading as if each raindrop were a citizen filing its own report.

features.name: passenger-observations
features.desc-on-touch: Notes from journeys through changing weather. One observer describes arriving wet; another, leaving dry. The archive notes the same storm, witnessed differently.

features.name: the-light-before-falling
features.desc-on-touch: Golden warmth in dust and air. The moment the sky holds its own weight and hasn't yet let go. The archive preserves this because it is always about to end.

inscription: the origin of rain is the moment before it remembers to fall; the archive keeps that moment in all its versions

file.name: Maitland_Line_Passenger_Weather_Log_1987_1991.txt
file.note: Dust-damage record of atmospheric observations from regional railway. Entries describe same storm from different carriages, same journey twice. Archive reconciles none of it.

exits.label: down to the cistern where all this rain will gather and know itself
upward to the clouds' long thinking
toward other stations where the same rain is falling differently
